inputs.label: Probability of Event A
inputs.businessContext: Base probability of event A occurring. Used in basic probability calculations. Referenced from probability theory fundamentals.

inputs.label: Probability of Event B
inputs.businessContext: Base probability of event B occurring. Used in joint and conditional probability calculations.

inputs.label: Conditional Probability P(B|A)
inputs.businessContext: Probability of B given A. Required for Bayes' theorem and conditional probability analysis.

inputs.label: Calculation Mode
inputs.businessContext: Select the type of probability calculation: joint (P(A∩B)), conditional (P(A|B)), Bayes (P(A|B) from P(B|A)), complement (P(not A)), union (P(A∪B)).

inputs.label: Data Confidence Level
inputs.businessContext: Confidence level in input data (0 to 1). Used to adjust output for uncertainty. Typical values: 0.9, 0.95, 0.99.

rules: If calculationMode is 'conditional' or 'bayes', eventAProbability must be > 0
If calculationMode is 'bayes', conditionalProbability must be provided

thresholds.eventAProbability: if eventAProbability < 0.01 then 'Very low probability event A'
thresholds.eventBProbability: if eventBProbability < 0.01 then 'Very low probability event B'
thresholds.conditionalProbability: if conditionalProbability < 0.01 then 'Very low conditional probability'

hiddenLossDrivers: Low probability values may indicate rare events requiring special attention
Low data confidence reduces reliability of results
suggestedActions: Verify input probabilities with historical data
Increase sample size to improve data confidence
Use sensitivity analysis to assess impact of probability variations
